name: The Peugh Law Firm

notableCases.name: Cole's Case - Possession of Marijuana record clearing (anonymized client result published by firm)
notableCases.outcome: Dismissal with conditions secured; after waiting period, expunction granted and record cleared

notableCases.name: Taylor's Case - Felony Possession of a Controlled Substance (anonymized client result published by firm)
notableCases.outcome: Offer of 12 months of felony probation

websiteQuality.rationale: The site is HTTPS-secured, mobile-ready (viewport meta present, Divi WordPress theme), with a proper title/meta description, clear navigation into dedicated practice-area pages, a working contact form, clickable tel: links, a published email address, footer address, and a fresh 2026 copyright. Trust signals include AVVO, Martindale peer-rating and Super Lawyers badges plus testimonials, though some badge assets are dated (alt text 'Peer Rated 2021'). Weaknesses: no online appointment booking/scheduler, heavy WordPress page payload, and a template-driven look. Solid but not best-in-class, hence Adequate.

factors.mobileUsability: Viewport meta tag present (width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0); responsive Divi theme; mobile usability appears supported.
factors.pageSpeed: Not directly measured (no lab tooling). Inferred as mediocre-to-average: homepage HTML alone is ~359 KB with ~22 script tags (WordPress/Divi + plugin assets), suggesting a moderately heavy page.
factors.accessibility: Images observed carry alt attributes (e.g. attorney photo, badge images); no accessibility statement; form fields use labels/required-field markers.
factors.visualCredibility: Professional but template-based (Divi child theme); consistent branding, attorney photos, and badge imagery; some badge assets dated 2021.
factors.navigation: Clear top nav (<nav id="top-menu-nav">) with About, dedicated practice-area pages (DWI, juvenile, drug, assault, theft/fraud, probation violation, record clearing), FAQ, News, Recent Results, Contact.
factors.contentClarity: Extensive, plainly written content: practice-area explainers, FAQs, blog/news posts, and an anonymized recent-results section; messaging consistently emphasizes former-prosecutor experience.
factors.callsToAction: Repeated 'free consultation' CTAs, prominent phone number with tel: links, email link, and an email-capture lead magnet ('Five Questions to Avoid Hiring the Wrong Criminal Defense Attorney').
factors.contactFormQuality: Contact page hosts a working form (Name required, Email, Phone, Description of Legal Issue) plus a separate email-capture form; free consultation offered.
factors.localPositioning: Strongly Denton-localized: footer address 721 W Mulberry Street, Denton, TX 76201; copy targets Denton County courts, prosecutors, and judges throughout.
factors.trustSignals: AVVO rating 10.0 badge, Martindale-Hubbell Peer Rated and Client Champion badges, Super Lawyers selections 2021-2023 and 2025-2026 (per Super Lawyers profile), client testimonials, former-prosecutor credentials.
factors.appointmentBooking: No online scheduler (no Calendly/Acuity/etc. detected); booking is via phone, email, or contact form.

sources.supports: firm name, phone 940-566-0271, footer address 721 W Mulberry Street Denton TX 76201, published email info@peughlawfirm.com, practice areas, CTAs, contact form, trust badges, 2026 copyright

office address 721 W Mulberry St., Denton, TX 76201; admitted to practice 1998; Super Lawyers selections 2021-2023, 2025-2026; practice focus criminal defense / DUI-DWI / white collar
secondary brand Mulberry Street Probate, Wills & Estates run by the same attorney Daniel K. Peugh; client testimonials; AVVO/Martindale badges
notableCases: anonymized firm-published results (Cole's Case expunction; Taylor's Case felony probation offer)
additional firm-published anonymized case results (record non-disclosure, expunction, probation outcomes)
directory corroboration: former Denton County ADA, private practice history, criminal-defense focus
Denton County speaker bio corroborating Texas bar 1998, Chief Juvenile Prosecutor 2000-2002, Chief Misdemeanor Prosecutor tenure

unverified: year firm established (no founding year stated on site or directories; attorney's prior partnership Tackett & Peugh LP ran Jan 2002-Jul 2009, so solo firm likely dates from 2009, but this is not stated publicly)
firm headcount beyond the named attorney (testimonials mention a paralegal 'Cara'; no other attorneys listed)
notes: Seed corrections: the seed's 'former Chief Prosecutor Denton County Criminal DA's Office' is more precisely former Chief Juvenile Prosecutor (2000-2002) and former Chief Misdemeanor Prosecutor (1999-2000); '20+ years' is now stated as 25+ years on the current homepage (Texas bar 1998). Secondary brand: the same attorney operates probateattorneydenton.com as 'Mulberry Street Probate, Wills & Estates' / 'Law Office of Dan Peugh' from the same Mulberry Street office - recorded here per instructions, not treated as a separate firm. Prior firm: Tackett & Peugh, L.P. (founding partner, 2002-2009). Fetch note: peughlawfirm.com returned 403 to the FetchURL tool for some pages; content was verified via direct curl fetch with a browser user-agent (HTTP 200) and search snippets; Justia profile page also returned 403.
